living-concrete.pattern: Always `final`. Never `var` unless mutation is genuinely needed. `final items = <String>[];` — trust inference, declare immutability. LLMs default to `var` or over-qualify: `final List<String> items = []`.
`const` constructors on immutable classes. `const` values where possible. LLMs frequently miss const opportunities.
No `dynamic`. Prefer `Object?` when truly unknown. Trust inference for locals: `final x = foo();` not `final Foo x = foo();`. Explicit types on public API signatures.

`final class` by default (closed for extension). `sealed class` for ADTs (exhaustive switch). `interface class` when contract matters. LLMs default to plain `class` — always choose the tightest modifier.

Records for lightweight tuples: `(int, String)` not custom Pair class. Pattern matching in switch: exhaustive, expression-form preferred. Extension types for zero-cost wrappers. Enhanced enums with members over enum + switch.
living-concrete.antipattern: try { op(); } catch (e) { log('fail'); return null; } // hides bugs try { fs.del(); } catch (e) { throw FsEx(e.str); } // loses stack
living-concrete.pattern: "Process [event] with [fflags]" (one-line, embedded params). Skip docs on private members and obvious methods entirely. LLMs over-document — silence is often the right doc.
living-concrete.antipattern: processExecutionManager — compress it. Multi-line doc on obvious getter — skip it. print() — use Logger. `var` where `final` works. Plain `class` where `final class` fits.
Hundreds of trivial tests — test BEHAVIOR not structure. Mixed setup/behavior/verify — separate phases clearly. Comments over reason: param — use reason:.
